Title: Intracellular polyamines regulate redox homeostasis with cAMP–PKA signalling during sexual mating/filamentation and pathogenicity of Sporisorium scitamineum

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduced sexual mating/filamentation, abolished pathogenicity, and altered polyamine/redox homeostasis, indicating contribution to putrescine supply upstream of spermidine accumulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal sexual mating/filamentation and pathogenicity; polyamine homeostasis affects intracellular redox balance.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In infected sugarcane stems, especially diseased whip/top stem for spermidine gradient
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated during early sugarcane infection at 3 and 7 dpi.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated during early sugarcane infection at 3 and 7 dpi.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; pathogenicity assay; mating/filamentation assay; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; intracellular H2O2 measurement; oxidative stress assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter used for gene context; no heterologous promoter analysis reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target to reduce sugarcane smut virulence by disrupting fungal polyamine homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption broadly alters metabolism and redox homeostasis; may have pleiotropic effects beyond polyamines.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative stress (H2O2); sugarcane infection; exogenous putrescine/spermidine/spermine supplementation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq mapped to GenBank assembly GCA_900002365.1; DEG datasets reported as Dataset S1 and S2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsAGA encodes a putative arginase in the fungal polyamine pathway. 2. Deletion reduced dikaryotic hyphal formation after mating and caused complete loss of pathogenicity on sugarcane. 3. SsAGA was down-regulated during early infection and its disruption was associated with elevated ROS and reduced oxidative stress tolerance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SsAGA contributes to polyamine-dependent development, growth on minimal medium was not strongly affected, suggesting it is less critical than SsODC/SsSAMDC for basal growth under tested conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; loss caused complete mating/filamentation defect and strong depletion of putrescine/spermidine in double mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion caused almost complete loss of dikaryotic hyphae after sexual mating, abolished pathogenicity, increased intracellular ROS, and was required for putrescine/spermidine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamines support mating/filamentation, virulence, and redox homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In infected sugarcane stems, spermidine enriched toward whip/top stem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly up-regulated in cAMP–PKA mutants ss1gpa3Δ, ss1uac1Δ, and ss1adr1Δ.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated in cAMP–PKA mutants ss1gpa3Δ, ss1uac1Δ, and ss1adr1Δ.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; complementation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; mating/filamentation assay; pathogenicity assay; RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; double-mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate antifungal target for controlling sugarcane smut by blocking fungal polyamine biosynthesis and virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation causes broad developmental and redox defects; high putrescine itself suppresses filamentation, indicating dosage-sensitive outcomes.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous putrescine/spermidine/spermine; cAMP treatment; oxidative stress (H2O2); sugarcane infection
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS polyamine profiling; RNA-seq context in study mapped to GenBank assembly GCA_900002365.1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsODC encodes ornithine decarboxylase required for fungal putrescine production. 2. Deletion caused near-complete loss of sexual mating/filamentation and loss of pathogenicity. 3. SsODC directly interacted with cAMP–PKA component SsGPA3 and linked polyamine metabolism to redox homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SsODC formed a homodimer in Y2H; deletion of SsODC in ss1uac1Δ produced abundant pseudohyphae/constitutive filamentous phenotype under control conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates S-adenosylmethionine to provide aminopropyl donor for spermidine biosynthesis from putrescine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for spermidine biosynthesis established by mutant rescue with spermidine and infection-induced expression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion impaired growth on minimal medium, reduced mating/filamentation, abolished pathogenicity, and demonstrated that SsSAMDC is critical for spermidine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Spermidine promotes fungal filamentation, virulence, and antioxidant/redox balance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Diseased whip/top region and upper infected sugarcane stem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated during early sugarcane infection at 3 and 7 dpi.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated during early sugarcane infection at 3 and 7 dpi.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; complementation; minimal-medium growth rescue by spermidine; LC-MS metabolite profiling; mating/filamentation assay; pathogenicity assay; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising antifungal target to suppress spermidine-dependent virulence in sugarcane smut fungus.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes broader metabolic rewiring including amino acid, fatty acid, secondary metabolism, and autophagy-related transcript changes.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous spermidine/spermine supplementation; oxidative stress (H2O2); sugarcane infection
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq mapped to GenBank assembly GCA_900002365.1; Dataset S1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsSAMDC is essential for spermidine biosynthesis, as loss caused a growth defect on minimal medium rescued by spermidine. 2. Deletion reduced mating/filamentation and eliminated pathogenicity on sugarcane. 3. SsSAMDC was induced during early infection, consistent with spermidine-promoted virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also monitored SsSPDS/SPMS expression, suggesting downstream aminopropyl transfer contributes to infection-associated spermidine accumulation, but SsSPDS/SPMS was not a primary experimental core gene.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SsGPA3 up-regulated SsODC and increased intracellular putrescine; spermidine supplementation partially restored mating/filamentation defect.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Polyamine biosynthesis and sexual mating/filamentation via cAMP–PKA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream controller of polyamine homeostasis affecting putrescine accumulation and filamentation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulation of SsODC-associated putrescine accumulation within cAMP–PKA signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Loss of SsGPA3 caused strong up-regulation of SsODC (6.48-fold).
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Cross-talk with polyamine pathway supported; feedback not directly established.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in cAMP–PKA signaling module with SsUAC1 and SsADR1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; RT-qPCR; LC-MS polyamine profiling; mating rescue by exogenous spermidine; yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling target to interfere with fungal virulence-associated polyamine regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Central signaling functions likely create pleiotropic effects beyond polyamine metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamine balance supports fungal pathogenic development and redox control.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected sugarcane stems; spermidine enriched toward whip/top stem
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsGPA3 is an upstream cAMP–PKA signaling component influencing polyamine metabolism. 2. Its deletion elevated SsODC expression and intracellular putrescine. 3. SsGPA3 physically interacted with SsODC, linking signaling to metabolic control of filamentation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exogenous spermidine, but not cAMP pathway bypass at prf1 mutant level, partially restored filamentation defects, placing polyamine action downstream or parallel to upstream cAMP components.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SsUAC1 up-regulated SsODC, increased intracellular putrescine, and spermidine supplementation restored mating/filamentation; double deletion with SsODC generated abundant pseudohyphae.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Polyamine homeostasis coupled to cAMP–PKA-dependent mating/filamentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking cAMP production to putrescine balance and filamentation outcome.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative control of SsODC-associated putrescine accumulation through cAMP signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Loss of SsUAC1 caused 1.80-fold up-regulation of SsODC.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Cross-talk with polyamine pathway supported; no direct feedback loop formally established.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in cAMP–PKA signaling module with SsGPA3 and SsADR1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: SsUAC1 transcript was down-regulated in ss1odcΔ × ss2odcΔ mating cultures.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; RT-qPCR; LC-MS polyamine profiling; exogenous spermidine rescue; double-mutant analysis; cAMP treatment assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for anti-virulence strategies via disruption of signaling-metabolism coupling.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Core signaling role suggests pleiotropic developmental effects.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous spermidine/spermine; cAMP treatment; oxidative stress measurement in double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamine balance supports fungal development, virulence, and redox homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected sugarcane stems; spermidine enriched toward whip/top stem
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsUAC1 acts upstream of polyamine homeostasis in the cAMP–PKA pathway. 2. Deletion elevated putrescine via increased SsODC expression and impaired filamentation that could be rescued by spermidine. 3. Combined deletion of SsUAC1 and SsODC caused pseudohyphal/filamentous morphology, highlighting strong pathway cross-talk.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct Y2H interaction with SsODC was detected.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SsADR1 strongly up-regulated SsODC, elevated intracellular putrescine, and spermidine supplementation restored mating/filamentation defect.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Polyamine biosynthesis/homeostasis during mating and filamentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream signaling regulator affecting putrescine accumulation through SsODC expression.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulation of SsODC-associated putrescine accumulation in cAMP–PKA context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Loss of SsADR1 caused 8.44-fold up-regulation of SsODC.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in cAMP–PKA signaling module with SsGPA3 and SsUAC1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; RT-qPCR; LC-MS polyamine profiling; exogenous spermidine rescue; cAMP response assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible anti-virulence signaling target affecting downstream polyamine balance.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic because of central developmental signaling role.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamine homeostasis supports fungal filamentation, virulence, and redox control.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected sugarcane stems; spermidine enriched toward whip/top stem
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsADR1 is a cAMP–PKA pathway regulator affecting fungal polyamine metabolism. 2. Deletion markedly increased SsODC expression and putrescine levels. 3. Spermidine could partially restore the developmental defect, linking SsADR1 to polyamine-mediated filamentation control.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct Y2H interaction with SsODC was detected.
